title: DayZ
notes: Stable branch is not working, probably because of the use of BattleEye Anti-Cheat. But in the experimental branch and started in offline-mode (alternative start option of DayZ) it is working just fine with around 70% of native performance.

title: DayZ
notes:
Proof: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ba8FxYJC-pw
Performance: Performs about 50% worse
Workarounds required: https://github.com/Jacob-Mango/DayZ-Linux

title: DayZ
notes:
***Only DayZ Offline mode on `experimental - Unstable testing version` branch is working***
- The game in offline mode works pretty good and out of the box.
- Performance is mediocre if not terrible (but I don't have Windows atm to compare how bad native performance is ;))
- Fullscreen/windowed mode works
- Alt tabbing works (sometimes in windowed mode after minimizing you have to alttab in and out to make it catch the correct resolution)
- Steam Overlay and Screenshots work
- But: USB mouse is terribly "heavy", it moves veeery slowly (like with very low acceleration, sens. in settings doesn't do much)
- What's weird - touchpad mouse works just fine
- Windowed mode runs slightly better, but there is no borderless option
- You can kinda fix mouse with: 
env WINEPREFIX="/..../steamapps/compatdata/221100/pfx/" WINEPATH="/..../steamapps/common/Proton 3.7/dist/bin/wine64" winecfg
And in "Wine Configuration" -> "Graphics" tab ->  enable "Automatically capture the mouse in full-screen windows" + enable "Virtual Desktop" (e.g. 1920x1080) and mouse after this kinda works fine -> Apply
- I haven't found zombies during ~30 minutes but suicide works :v Don't know how performance would be on native Windows but it's pretty bad here
- Trying to play "normal" (online) DayZ fails with Battleye error "Failed to initialize BattlEye Service: Driver Load Error (31)"

To sum up: Decided to call it "Stable" because it, well, works. But only experimental offline version, performance is mediocre and there are some fixable mouse problems.

title: DayZ

notes: Latest BETA 0.63 STABLE branch (2018-11-07). Won't start with any launch option (even Offline/Single player). Sometimes shows an error: "Out of memory (requested xxx KB). footprint xxxxxxxxx KB". Normal launch gets stuck on the BattlEye initialization.
title: DayZ

notes: 0.63.149653 (Experimental branch) (2018-12-03). Won't start with any launch option (even Offline/Single player). Sometimes shows an error: "Out of memory (requested xxx KB). footprint xxxxxxxxx KB". Normal launch gets stuck on the BattlEye initialization.
title: DayZ

notes: 1.0.149923 (Stable branch) (2018-12-14). Won't start even without BattlEye (cancel license agreement&setup). Sometimes shows an error: "Out of memory (requested xxx KB). footprint xxxxxxxxx KB". Normal launch gets stuck on the BattlEye initialization.
title: DayZ

notes:
So DayZ recently released as 1.0. It did not improved gaming on Linux sadly. On current stable version game fails to run due to BattlEye. On first run it fails with ```
Installing BattlEye Service...
Successfully installed BattlEye Service.
Failed to initialize BattlEye Service: Driver Load Error (31).
``` error. On consecutive runs it hangs on `Starting BattlEye Service...` information. That's for `Play DayZ` option. For `Run DayZ Launcher` nothing happens and game quits shortly after. By game I mean Wine processes as no game window is shown with either option. That's unfortunate that it is another game borked by BattlEye, as DayZ (experimental branch) offline mode worked quite well.
title: DayZ

notes:
1.0.150000 (Stable branch) (2018-12-22). Won't get to the main menu even without BattlEye (cancel license agreement&setup). Shows the loading screen for a few seconds and crashes.
Sometimes shows an error: "Out of memory (requested xxx KB). footprint xxxxxxxxx KB". 
Normal launch gets stuck on the BattlEye initialization after you've accepted the BattlEye license agreement.
